assertions.description: The top-level multi-sheet parse correctly identifies CAN bus nets: CAN_TX and CAN_RX connecting to STM32 U201, and CAN_TXD/CAN_RXD on the transceiver side, plus physical CAN_L and CAN_H. The MCP1754ST LDO (U301) is correctly identified as an LDO with +5V input and +3V3 output. The 8 MHz crystal circuit with 20 pF load caps for the STM32 is also correctly detected.

INCORRECT: bus_analysis.i2c lists net '+3V3' as an SDA line with devices U201, U301, and U401. This is a clear false positive: +3V3 is a power supply rail, not a data bus. The misclassification occurs because U201 (STM32F072) has a pin named 'PB11/I2C2_SDA/...' that happens to connect to the +3V3 rail (likely a power pin group or the schematic wires the pin to VCC). The analyzer is matching I2C bus membership based on partial pin-name substring rather than requiring the net to not be a power rail.

INCORRECT: bus_analysis.i2c lists net 'CAN_S' as an SCL line with device U201. CAN_S is the silent/standby control pin of the TJA1051 CAN transceiver (U401), not an I2C clock line. U201's PB6 pin name includes 'I2C1_SCL' among its alternate functions but the net is named CAN_S and carries a CAN bus signal. The analyzer should suppress I2C bus detection when the net name strongly indicates a different protocol (e.g. 'CAN_S').
